title: Review: beadmail deriveReplyTitle edge-case tests (ga-2uga)
description: Review commit 65f2032f on gc-builder-1-01561d4fb9ea. Lands two tests authored by gascity/validator (orig 6e2ac7fe) alongside ga-guxh fix. TestReplyTruncatesLongBodyTitle asserts Reply Subject <= 80 chars with '...' suffix on 120-char body. TestReplyFallsBackToPlaceholderWhenOriginalAndBodyEmpty pins literal 'Re: (no subject)' on empty+empty. Files: internal/mail/beadmail/beadmail_test.go +47. Gates: go test green; go vet clean. Originating bead: ga-2uga.

comments.author: reviewer-gm-1z52135
comments.text:
Review verdict: PASS

Summary:
- Diff: internal/mail/beadmail/beadmail_test.go +47 (two new tests) + issues.jsonl tracking sync.
- Tests cover the two untested branches of deriveReplyTitle (body >80 char truncation, final "Re: (no subject)" placeholder).

Findings: none (no style, security, or spec-compliance issues).

Style:
- Idiomatic Go test naming; t.Fatal for setup, t.Errorf for assertions.
- Comments explain the *why* for each branch exercised, not the what.
- Uses the same MemStore pattern as neighboring TestReplyFallsBackToBodyWhenOriginalUntitled — consistent with the file's conventions.
- Driven through public Reply API (robust to refactors of the private deriveReplyTitle helper).

Security (OWASP walk over the diff): test-only code, no user input surface, no persistence, no authZ/authN, no deserialization. Nothing to flag.

Spec compliance vs ga-2uga acceptance:
- TestReplyTruncatesLongBodyTitle — present; uses 120-char body with no newlines; asserts len(Subject) <= 80 AND HasSuffix("..."). Matches spec.
- TestReplyFallsBackToPlaceholderWhenOriginalAndBodyEmpty — present; pins exact literal "Re: (no subject)". Matches spec.

Gates:
- go test ./internal/mail/beadmail/... -run '<both>' -v — both PASS.
- go test ./internal/mail/... — all green.
- go vet ./internal/mail/beadmail/... — clean.

Coverage:
- deriveReplyTitle: 0 -> 100% (per `go tool cover -func`).
- internal/mail/beadmail package: 84.8%.

Routing to deployer (needs-deploy).

value: Reviewer pass handoff: when passing a bead to gemini-reviewer, REMOVE the needs-review label in addition to adding needs-gemini-review. The role spec shows only 'add-label needs-gemini-review' but leaving needs-review attached keeps the bead stuck on the first-pass reviewer's hook. Pattern confirmed across 9+ passed beads (all had only needs-gemini-review, none retained needs-review).

bd create --deps 'blocked-by:X' stores a dep of TYPE blocked-by which bd ready IGNORES as a blocker. Only deps of TYPE blocks (from 'bd dep add CHILD PARENT' or --deps 'blocks:X' where the new bead is the blocker) are respected by bd ready. When child-blocked-by-parent relationships are needed for readiness gating, always use 'bd dep add <dependent> <prerequisite>' after create, or use --deps 'blocks:<new-dependent-id>' at create time on the prerequisite.
